raw_consumer_statement: Hello. On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, I received a message from my bank asking to verify a transaction for {$2200.00} at XXXX XXXX. I answered " No '' immediately as I was in my house which is in XXXXXXXX XXXX and realized that my wallet was lost ( Which included my driver 's license and Citi Credit Card ) I quickly called the bank and reported that, and we found out about other transactions already posted ( XXXX $ + $ XXXX $ XXXX $ XXXX {$4100.00} ) totaling more than {$16000.00}. All these transactions were made in XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX  I live in XXXX. I waited almost XXXX months for my bank to reach out to me the first time after the disputes were made. They told me that they would not be continuing some kind of investigation without any police reports, and also, they also wanted me to send them my new Driver 's License, they told me if I still did not get the new one, I should do it and send it to them too. I asked them why they wanted it after XXXX months and if they could have just asked for it at the beginning when I opened the disputes. That is because at the beginning I was asking them what they needed me to do to help them they answered they did not need anything and that if they did they would tell me. After XXXX months, they asked me why I did not get my police report in a very bad and surprised manner. That is ridiculous! I asked them why those transactions amounting to more than $ XXXX went through without them contacting me first. I have other credit cards that I try to put gas in my car with just XXXX miles away from my house which is an unusual activity, the bank immediately calls me to verify it before letting that transaction go through. Now, my card was used in a different state they did not even bother to verify those huge amounts in an unusual zip code. They said just sorry about it. Long story short, I sent whatever they needed the next business day, the police report along with my new DL, and the same day they told me they had denied my claim again and that I was responsible for those charges.
